revision.text:
[[ja:眉庇付冑]] [[File:KofunHelmet.jpg|Thumbnail|Gold-copper-plated riveted helmet with brow plate (Exhibited at the {{ill|Tokyo National Museum|en|Tokyo National Museum|qid=Q653433|lt=Tokyo National Museum|1=Tokyo National Museum}})]] [[File:Helmet MET DT305559.jpg|thumb|220px|right|Gold-copper-plated riveted helmet with brow plate from the {{ill|Sakume Ōtsukayama Kofun|ja|佐久米古墳群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakume Ōtsukayama Kofun|1=Sakume Kofun Group|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q92453561}} (Exhibited at the {{ill|Metropolitan Museum of Art|en|Metropolitan Museum of Art|qid=Q160236|lt=Metropolitan Museum of Art|1=Metropolitan Museum of Art}})]] {{Nihongo|'''Helmet with Brow Plate'''|眉庇付冑|mabisashi-tsuki kabuto}} is a type of {{ill|Kabuto|en|Kabuto|qid=Q1720878|lt=Kabuto (helmet)|1=Kabuto}} crafted and used during the mid-{{ill|Kofun period|en|Kofun period|qid=Q459939|lt=Kofun period|1=Kofun period}} in Japan. It is one of the representative types of {{ill|Japanese armour|en|Japanese armour|qid=Q3671179|lt=Armor and helmets (katchū)|1=Japanese armour}} discovered as burial goods in {{ill|Kofun|en|Kofun|qid=Q1141225|lt=Kofun|1=Kofun}}. These helmets were used in combination with {{ill|Tankō|en|Tankō|qid=Q1687003|lt=Tankō|1=Tankō}} or {{ill|Lamellar armour|lt=Lamellar armour|lt=Keikō (lamellar armour)|ja|小札甲|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Lamellar armour|1=Lamellar armour|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q131123788}} from the mid-{{ill|5th century|en|5th century|qid=Q8095|lt=5th century|1=5th century}} to the early {{ill|6th century|en|6th century|qid=Q8090|lt=6th century|1=6th century}}.{{Sfn|Suenaga|1944}} == Overview == The name "helmet with brow plate" derives from the large "brow plate" affixed to the front of the round hemispherical body (bowl) of the helmet, resembling the brim of a baseball cap.{{Sfn|Suenaga|1934}}{{Sfn|Suenaga|1944}} The helmets were generally made of iron, though some were crafted from gold-copper. This design appeared slightly later than the {{ill|Horned Kabuto|ja|衝角付冑|1=Horned Kabuto|lt=Horned helmet|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Horned Kabuto|13=User:Immanuelle/Horned Kabuto|qq=}}, emerging in the early to mid-5th century and persisting until the early 6th century.{{Sfn|Tanaka|2003}} === Structure === * '''Bowl (鉢):''' The helmet's bowl was constructed by connecting small iron plates, known as kozane, or long, curved iron bands in a circular shape. Two bands were wrapped at the lower and middle sections (waist plate and body plate). The top was covered with a circular iron plate (伏板). A hemispherical ornament (伏鉢 and 受鉢) was mounted on top, connected with a tubular fitting (管). These fittings likely held decorative elements such as fibers or animal hair. A unique example featuring branch-like metal ornaments was discovered at the {{ill|Inadō Kofun Cluster|ja|稲童古墳群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inadō Kofun Cluster|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in Fukuoka Prefecture.{{Sfn|Yukuhashi City Board of Education|2015}} * '''Shikoro (錣):''' This was a series of overlapping iron bands suspended from the back of the bowl to protect the nape of the neck, secured with leather cords. * '''Brow Plate (眉庇):''' The defining feature of this helmet, the brow plate was a wide iron plate resembling a fan. Many had openwork designs, classified into leaf, lens, or triangular patterns. === Manufacturing Techniques === Unlike the horned helmet, which used leather threading to join plates, helmets with brow plates were crafted using riveting techniques, where iron rivets secured the plates.{{Sfn|Sakaguchi|2001}} == Variations == Based on construction and technique, these helmets are broadly classified as follows:{{Sfn|Tanaka|2003}} * '''Gold-copper-plated riveted helmet with brow plate (小札鋲留眉庇付冑):''' Made of small iron plates fastened with rivets. * '''Horizontal riveted helmet with brow plate (横矧板鋲留眉庇付冑):''' Constructed from elongated iron bands similar to the body plates, joined with rivets. === Other Types === '''Mongol-style Bowl Helmet with Brow Plate:''' A helmet from the {{ill|Gojō Nekozuka Kofun|ja|五条猫塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gojō Nekozuka Kofun|1=Gojō Nekozuka Kofun|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11372490}} in Nara Prefecture is notable for its pronounced upward curvature, resembling Mongol-style helmets found in Korea and China. == Gallery == 鶴山古墳 (太田市) 出土 眉庇付冑.JPG|Helmet with brow plate from {{ill|Tsuru Kofun|lt=Tsuru Kofun|lt=Tsuruyama Kofun|ja|鶴山古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuruyama Kofun|qq=}}
{{small|Gunma Prefecture. Displayed at the Gunma Prefectural Museum.}} 祇園大塚山古墳出土 金銅製眉庇付冑 (J-728).JPG|Helmet with brow plate from {{ill|Gion Ōtsukayama Kofun|ja|祇園大塚山古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gion Ōtsukayama Kofun|qq=}}
{{small|Chiba Prefecture. Displayed at the Tokyo National Museum.}} 二本松山古墳出土 金銅装眉庇付冑 (J-14168).JPG|Helmet with brow plate from {{ill|Nihonmatsuyama Kofun|ja|二本松山古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nihonmatsuyama Kofun|qq=}}
{{small|Fukui Prefecture. Displayed at the Tokyo National Museum.}} == Notes == {{NoteHelp}} {{Reflist|group=Note}} == References == {{Reflist}} == Sources == * {{Cite book|author=Suenaga, Masao|title=Japanese Armor of the Early Period|date=1934|publisher=[[Okashoin|ja|岡書院]]}} * {{Cite journal|author=Sakaguchi, Hideki|title=Types and Transformations of Armor|journal=Archaeology Quarterly|volume=76|publisher=[[Yūzankaku|ja|雄山閣]]|year=2001}} == See Also == * {{ill|Kabuto|en|Kabuto|qid=Q1720878|lt=Kabuto (helmet)|1=Kabuto}} * {{ill|Japanese armour|en|Japanese armour|qid=Q3671179|lt=Japanese armor and helmets|1=Japanese armour}} * {{ill|Tankō|en|Tankō|qid=Q1687003|lt=Tankō|1=Tankō}} * {{ill|Lamellar armour|lt=Keikō (lamellar armour)|ja|挂甲|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Lamellar armour|1=keikō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11077681}} * {{ill|Horned helmet|ja|衝角付冑|1=Horned Kabuto|lt=Horned helmet|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Horned helmet|13=User:Immanuelle/Horned Kabuto|qq=}} * {{ill|Kofun period|en|Kofun period|qid=Q459939|lt=Kofun period|1=Kofun period}} {{translated page|ja|眉庇付冑|version=104626710|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:眉庇付冑]] {{wikidata link|Q48748019}} [[Category:Helmets]] [[Category:Kabuto]] [[Category:Kofun period]] [[Category:Military history of Japan]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]]
